title: Festuca atlantigena
taxonomy: Festuca atlantigena is classified as a species of grass within the genus Festuca, which is a group of temperate grasses native to the Northern Hemisphere. The genus Festuca is part of the family Poaceae, commonly known as the grass family. This classification places Festuca atlantigena within the subfamily Pooideae, which is a large and diverse group of grasses. The species itself is further classified as being part of the section Festuca, which is characterized by its leaf and spikelet morphology. As a result, the full classification of Festuca atlantigena reflects its position within the broader plant kingdom.

properties: Festuca atlantigena is known for its traditional use in folk medicine, particularly in the Iberian Peninsula. intro: Festuca atlantigena, also known as Atlantic fescue, is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful for treating various ailments such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Festuca atlantigena include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Atlantic fescue include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are often used in traditional medicine to support health and well-being.
